title: ORM Bookstore CRUD with Mongoose
statement:
In this challenge, you will implement a basic CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) API for a bookstore using Mongoose as the ORM (Object Relational Mapping) tool. The bookstore will have books with titles and authors. Your API should support getting all books, getting a book by its ID, creating a new book, updating an existing book, and deleting a book. Please implement the required endpoints and follow the provided starter code. You can assume the database connection is already established and the Mongoose models are ready to use.

### API Endpoints:

#### GET /api/books
Get all books in the bookstore.

#### GET /api/books/:id
Get a book by its ID.

#### POST /api/books
Create a new book.

#### PUT /api/books/:id
Update a book by its ID.

#### DELETE /api/books/:id
Delete a book by its ID.

### Request and Response Bodies:

#### GET /api/books
* Request: None
* Response: JSON list of book objects with title and author properties

#### GET /api/books/:id
* Request: None
* Response: JSON book object with title and author properties

#### POST /api/books
* Request: JSON book object with title and author properties
* Response: JSON book object with ID, title, and author properties

#### PUT /api/books/:id
* Request: JSON book object with title and author properties
* Response: JSON book object with ID, title, and author properties

#### DELETE /api/books/:id
* Request: None
* Response: None (204 No Content)

### Constraints:

* You must use Mongoose as the ORM tool.
* You must handle errors and edge cases properly.
* You must follow RESTful API design principles.

### Starter Code:

The starter code is provided below. Please implement the required endpoints and follow the code structure.

### Judge Configuration:

The judge will spin up your code and execute the test cases against your API. Make sure to follow the provided starter code and implement the required endpoints correctly.

### Scoring:

Your code will be scored based on its correctness, performance, and code quality.
constraints: Must use Mongoose as the ORM tool.

Must follow RESTful API design principles.
